chief_complaint: A 75-year-old female patient presented with acute onset of polyuria, polydipsia, and weight loss over the past week.
history: The patient has a 10-year history of type 2 diabetes mellitus, managed with metformin and lifestyle modifications. She has no history of ketoacidosis or pancreatic autoantibodies. Her family history is unremarkable.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 100 bpm, RR 24/min, SpO2 98%. Physical examination revealed dry mucous membranes, decreased skin turgor, and a negative fundoscopic examination.
labs: Blood glucose: 420 mg/dL, HbA1c: 12.5%, pancreatic islet function: impaired, amylase levels: elevated, diabetic autoantibodies: negative. ECG: normal.
correct_diagnosis: Type 2 diabetes mellitus complicated with fulminant type 1 diabetes mellitus
differentials: Type 2 diabetes mellitus complicated with fulminant type 1 diabetes mellitus
Diabetic ketoacidosis due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
correct_ranking: Type 2 diabetes mellitus complicated with fulminant type 1 diabetes mellitus
Diabetic ketoacidosis due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
